params.name: Micrographia
params.description:
On May 23, 1665 AD, Hooke observed the honeycomb-like structure of cell walls through a microscope.
Micrographia is the first major publication of the Royal Society of London. With its exquisite copperplate engravings, it became a bestseller in its time. In the book, Robert Hooke coined the term "cell" to describe the small compartments he observed through the microscope. In addition to discussing microscope techniques, Hooke also incorporated his own optical theories into the book and person …
